verification.summary: GST launched July 1, 2017 via 101st Constitutional Amendment after building consensus with all states through the GST Council. Revenue grew from Rs 7.41L Cr (FY18) to Rs 22.09L Cr (FY25).

timeline.event: Constitution 122nd Amendment Bill introduced in Lok Sabha
101st Constitutional Amendment passed; GST Council formed with all state finance ministers
GST launched July 1 in midnight Parliament session; 4 GST Bills passed March 27
E-way bill system launched for inter-state movement of goods
Rate rationalization; simplified return filing
COVID impact; compensation cess dispute — Centre borrows Rs 1.10L Cr for states
State compensation period ended June 30; Centre clears all dues
Record single-month collection Rs 2.10L Cr (April 2024); FY24 gross Rs 20.18L Cr
FY25 gross Rs 22.09L Cr (9.4% YoY growth); 1.51 Cr active taxpayers; 2-tier restructuring proposed

verification.notes: Both GST promises (ECON-020 and ECON-030) refer to the same policy. State concerns were addressed through GST Council (one vote per state), 5-year compensation guarantee, and revenue-neutral rates. Compensation cess dispute (2020-22) was the main friction point.

verification.summary: GST implemented within 3 years (July 2017). GSTN (GST Network) — the robust IT system — handles 1.51 Cr taxpayers, processes Rs 22L Cr annually, and supports real-time invoice matching.

timeline.event: GSTN (Goods and Services Tax Network) incorporated as Section 8 company
GSTN IT infrastructure built; pilot testing with states
GSTN goes live July 1 handling registration, returns, payments for all states
E-way bill module launched on GSTN; return simplification
GSTN handles surge in digital filing during COVID lockdowns
GSTN processes 1.51 Cr active taxpayers; Invoice Management System (IMS) launched

verification.notes: This promise had two parts: (1) usher in GST — done July 2017, and (2) robust IT network — GSTN is one of the world's largest tax IT systems. See also BJP2014-ECON-020.

verification.summary: OROP implemented via MoD notification November 7, 2015. Pension equalized based on average of min/max of 2013 retirees. OROP-I: Rs 10,795 Cr arrears to 20.6 lakh beneficiaries. OROP-II (2023): Rs 23,638 Cr arrears to 25.13 lakh beneficiaries. Supreme Court upheld the scheme in March 2022.

timeline.event: OROP announced by PM Modi on Independence Day (August 15); MoD formal notification November 7
OROP-I arrears disbursement begins in 4 installments; Rs 10,795 Cr total arrears
Indian Ex-Servicemen Movement files Supreme Court petition challenging OROP formula (June 25)
Total OROP-I expenditure reaches Rs 42,740 Cr (arrears + recurring)
Supreme Court upholds OROP scheme (Justices Chandrachud & Surya Kant, March 16); rejects 'automatic revision' demand
OROP-II revision announced: Rs 23,638 Cr arrears, Rs 8,450 Cr/year recurring, 25.13 lakh beneficiaries

verification.caveats: OROP was implemented but the formula uses 'average of min/max pension' rather than 'highest pension' as ex-servicemen demanded. Revision is periodic (every 5 years) not automatic. Supreme Court upheld this approach. Some veteran groups consider it partial fulfillment.
verification.notes: The Koshyari Committee (Rajya Sabha, 2011) had recommended OROP with automatic revision. The government's version uses periodic revision instead. SC ruled Koshyari report is not binding.

promises.text: Ram Setu: Ram Setu is a part of our cultural heritage and also of strategic importance due to its vast thorium deposits. These facts will be taken into consideration while taking any decision on 'Sethu-Samudram Channel' project

promises.text: In addition, a massive 'Clean Rivers Programme' will be launched across the country driven by people's participation

promises.text: Heritage Sites: We will provide appropriate resources for the maintenance and restoration of all national heritage sites, and to prevent their vandalisation in any form. The digitization of archives and archeological and museological records would be initiated. A National Mission for beautifying and improving the infrastructure and amenities at pilgrimage centres of all faiths will be launched

A National Mission for beautifying and improving the infrastructure and amenities at pilgrimage centres of all faiths will be launched.

promises.text: Languages: Indian Languages are repositories of our rich literature, history, culture, art and scientific achievements. Many of our dialects are important source for knowing our heritage. BJP would promote Indian languages, and put measures for the development of all Indian languages, so that they become a powerful vehicle for creating a knowledge society

promises.text: BJP believes that there cannot be gender equality till such time India adopts a Uniform Civil Code, which protects the rights of all women, and the BJP reiterates its stand to draft a Uniform Civil Code, drawing upon the best traditions and harmonizing them with the modern times
